zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen於0933年8月28日出生在法國諾門迪費艾坎普時，他的父親Guillaume 'Longue-Épée' Comte de Rouen40歲，母親Sprota de Bretagne23歲。他於0972年12月26日與Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利費艾坎普結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於0996年11月21日在法國諾門迪費艾坎普享年63歲，埋葬在法國諾門迪。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen于0933年8月28日出生在法国諾門迪費艾坎普时，他的父亲Guillaume 'Longue-Épée' Comte de Rouen40岁，母亲Sprota de Bretagne23岁。他于0972年12月26日与Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利費艾坎普结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于0996年11月21日在法国諾門迪費艾坎普享年63岁，埋葬在法国諾門迪。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Normandie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Alain, Serge, Adrien, Andre, Aurore, Celine, Clovis, Emile, Gaetan, Gilles, Laurier.
French: from a diminutive of   Normand  . Compare   Normandy  .

variants.name: Normand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gaston, Andre, Laurent, Marcel, Pierre, Amie, Donat, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean Noel, Jean-Michel.
French and Breton (mainly Finistère; also Le Normand): habitational name for someone from Normandy, a region in northern France (see Norman ). Compare Lenormand .

variants.name: Normandy
meanings: English: habitational name from Normandy in northern France. This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen was born on 28 August 0933, in Fécamp, Normandy, France, his father, Guillaume 'Longue-Épée' Comte de Rouen, was 40 and his mother, Sprota de Bretagne, was 23. He married Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen on 26 December 0972, in Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 21 November 0996, in Fécamp, Normandy, France, at the age of 63, and was buried in Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen est né le 28 août 0933, à Fécamp, Normandie, France, son père, Guillaume 'Longue-Épée' Comte de Rouen, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Sprota de Bretagne, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen le 26 décembre 0972, à Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 21 novembre 0996, à Fécamp, Normandie, France, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen am 28. August 0933 in Fécamp, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guillaume 'Longue-Épée' Comte de Rouen 40 und seine Mutter Sprota de Bretagne 23. Er hat am 26. Dezember 0972 in Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 21. November 0996 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Fécamp, Normandie, Frankreich und wurde in Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen è nato il il 28 agosto 0933 nel Fécamp, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Guillaume 'Longue-Épée' Comte de Rouen, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Sprota de Bretagne, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen il 26 dicembre 0972, nel Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 21 novembre 0996, nel Fécamp, Normandie, Francia, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouenが0933年8月28日にフランスノーマンディフeカンップで生まれた時，父Guillaume 'Longue-Épée' Comte de Rouenは40歳，母Sprota de Bretagneは23歳であった。0972年12月26日にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメフeカンップでGunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は0996年11月21日にフランスノーマンディフeカンップで63歳で死去し，フランスノーマンディにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen(이)가 0933년 8월 28일 프랑스 노먼디 프e캠프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guillaume 'Longue-Épée' Comte de Rouen(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Sprota de Bretagne(은)는 23세였다. 그는 0972년 12월 26일 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 프e캠프에서 Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 0996년 11월 21일 프랑스 노먼디 프e캠프에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노먼디에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen nasceu em 28 de agosto de 0933, em Fécamp, Normandie, França, seu pai, Guillaume 'Longue-Épée' Comte de Rouen, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Sprota de Bretagne, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen em 26 de dezembro de 0972, em Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 21 de novembro de 0996, em Fécamp, Normandie, França, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen родился 28 августа 0933 г. в местности фeкамп, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Guillaume 'Longue-Épée' Comte de Rouen было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Sprota de Bretagne, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen 26 декабря 0972 г. в местности фeкамп, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 21 ноября 0996 г. в местности фeкамп, норманди, Франция в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности норманди, Франция.
